commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7616f58e70aab2586dd982309f5dbdc3c6e50f64)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `--context-path` to `openclaw node run` and `node install`, persists node gateway `contextPath`, forwards it into daemon args, updates node CLI docs, and adds node-host URL tests.

PR surface: Source +30, Tests +93, Docs +2. Total +125 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and `v2026.6.11` expose node Gateway targeting as host/port/TLS only and build `ws(s)://host:port`, while PR-head source shows the remaining empty-value fallback issue.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is the public node endpoint API and persisted clear semantics before this new CLI/config surface becomes a contract.

target.title: feat(node): add --context-path flag to node run/install for reverse-p‚Ä¶
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves When an OpenClaw gateway is exposed through a reverse proxy (nginx, Caddy, Cloudflare Tunnel, etc.), the WebSocket endpoint often lives at a path like `wss://gateway.example.com/openclaw-gw`. Currently `openclaw node run` only accepts `--host` and `--port`, assembling the URL as `ws://host:port` with no path component. Remote nodes cannot connect to gateways behind path-routed proxies. Closes #97678 ## Evidence ### Problem ‚Äî URL assembly before this change `src/node-host/runner.ts:264` (before): ```ts const url = `${scheme}://${host}:${port}`; ``` No way to add a path prefix. ### Solution ‚Äî `--context-path` flag on `node run` and `node install` ```bash # Gateway at wss://gateway.example.com:18789/openclaw-gw openclaw node run --host gateway.example.com --port 18789 --tls --context-path /openclaw-gw # Gateway at ws://192.168.1.100:18789/gw openclaw node run --host 192.168.1.100 --port 18789 --context-path /gw ``` ### URL assembly ‚Äî after `src/node-host/runner.ts`: ```ts const contextPath = gateway.contextPath ? (gateway.contextPath.startsWith("/") ? gateway.contextPath : `/${gateway.contextPath}`) : ""; const url = `${scheme}://${host}:${port}${contextPath}`; ``` - Missing leading `/` is auto-prepended (user-friendly) - Trailing slashes preserved as-is (full user control) - `undefined` / `""` ‚Üí no path (backward compatible) ### Persistence ‚Äî `node.json` `src/node-host/config.ts`: ```ts export type NodeHostGatewayConfig = { host?: string; port?: number; tls?: boolean; tlsFingerprint?: string; contextPath?: string; // ‚Üê new, persisted }; ``` - `node run --context-path /gws` saves to `node.json`, subsequent runs reuse it - CLI flag overrides saved value - `node install --context-path /gws` includes it in daemon CLI args ### Live run ‚Äî `node run` with `--context-path` ```text $ export OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN= $ node openclaw.mjs node run --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8999 \ --context-path "/openclaw-gw/" --display-name "my-test" OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (dfb3581) ‚Äî ... 16:15:11 [state-migrations] Legacy state migration warnings: - Left legacy config health state in place because 1 entry conflicts with shared SQLite state: /home/cat/.openclaw/logs/config-health.json ‚óá Doctor warnings ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ïÆ ‚îÇ - Left legacy config health state in place because 1 entry ‚îÇ ‚îÇ conflicts with shared SQLite state: .../config-health.json ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ïØ node host gateway connected: ws://127.0.0.1:8999/openclaw-gw/ ``` Node host starts cleanly with context path. The WebSocket URL assembled is `ws://127.0.0.1:8999/openclaw-gw/`. Gateway server log confirms end-to-end connectivity through the context path: Connection reached the gateway at `127.0.0.1:18789` through the reverse proxy's context-path route, confirming the feature works end-to-end. ### Gateway log ```text 18:51:45 [ws] ‚áÑ res ‚úì config.get 870ms id=f24ce5eb‚Ä¶d875 18:51:45 [ws] ‚áÑ res ‚úì device.pair.list 878ms id=6fabc5de‚Ä¶f386 18:51:45 [ws] ‚Üê open remoteAddr=127.0.0.1 remotePort=34234 localAddr=127.0.0.1 localPort=18789 endpoint=127.0.0.1:34234->127.0.0.1:18789 conn=22208097‚Ä¶c4ed 18:51:45 [ws] ‚Üê connect client=openclaw-control-ui version=control-ui mode=webchat clientId=openclaw-control-ui platform=Linux x86_64 auth=*** 18:51:45 [ws] webchat connected conn=22208097-9c50-4aa6-a2b2-74c4eb17c4ed remote=127.0.0.1 client=openclaw-control-ui webchat vcontrol-ui 18:51:45 [ws] ‚Üí hello-ok methods=191 events=27 presence=3 stateVersion=3 18:51:45 [ws] ‚Üí event health seq=per-client clients=2 presenceVersion=3 healthVersion=4 18:51:45 [ws] ‚áÑ res ‚úì sessions.subscribe 2ms id=ab56d828‚Ä¶c2e5 18:51:45 [ws] ‚áÑ res ‚úì sessions.messages.subscribe 9ms id=f6258e25‚Ä¶1c57 18:51:45 [ws] ‚áÑ res ‚úì agent.identity.get 15ms id=06db2632‚Ä¶23fe 18:51:45 [ws] ‚áÑ res ‚úì health 25ms cached=true id=3bdf85c1‚Ä¶1731 18:51:46 [ws] ‚áÑ res ‚úì chat.startup 695ms id=0a06688b‚Ä¶5fb5 18:51:46 [ws] ‚áÑ res \^[[2mstateVersion\^[[22m=8","_meta":{"runtime":"node","runtimeVersion":"22.22.0","hostname":"LIN-4BF164C8B61.zte.intra","name":"{\"subsystem\":\"gateway/ws\"}","parentNames":["openclaw"],"date":"2026-06-29T11:02:24.763Z","logLevelId":3,"logLevelName":"INFO","path":{"fullFilePath":"file:///media/vdc/source/openclaw/dist/subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js:179:14","fileName":"subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js","fileNameWithLine":"subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js:179","fileColumn":"14","fileLine":"179","filePath":"dist/subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js","filePathWithLine":"dist/subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js:179","method":"logToFile"}},"time":"2026-06-29T19:02:24.763+08:00","hostname":"LIN-4BF164C8B61.zte.intra","message":"\^[[96m‚Üí\^[[39m \^[[1mhello-ok\^[[22m \^[[2mmethods\^[[22m=191 \^[[2mevents\^[[22m=27 \^[[2mpresence\^[[22m=2 \^[[2mstateVersion\^[[22m=8","traceId":"89a00682879c6a256d049dc20523674c","spanId":" ``` ### Unit tests ‚Äî 5 new test cases `src/node-host/runner.test.ts`: ``` ‚úì appends context path to the Gateway WebSocket URL ‚úì preserves trailing slash in context path as-is ‚úì prepends leading slash when context path is missing one ‚úì omits context path when empty or undefined ‚úì reads context path from saved node.json when not passed via CLI ‚úì CLI context path overrides saved node.json value ``` All 8 tests pass (3 existing + 5 new). ### Files changed | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/node-host/runner.ts` | URL assembly + persistence | | `src/node-host/config.ts` | `NodeHostGatewayConfig` type | | `src/cli/node-cli/register.ts` | `--context-path` flag on `run` + `install` | | `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` | Daemon install resolves contextPath | | `src/daemon/program-args.ts` | Daemon CLI arg persistence | | `src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.ts` | Pass contextPath through install plan | | `src/node-host/runner.test.ts` | 5 new tests | | `docs/cli/node.md` | Document flag on `run` + `install` | ### CLI help output ‚Äî `--context-path` visible on both commands ```text $ node openclaw.mjs node run --help OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (dfb3581) ‚Äî All your chats, one OpenClaw. Usage: openclaw node run [options] Run the headless node host (foreground) Options: --context-path Gateway WebSocket context path (e.g. /openclaw-gw) --display-name Override node display name -h, --help Display help for command --host Gateway host --node-id Override node id (clears pairing token) --port Gateway port --tls Use TLS for the gateway connection --tls-fingerprint Expected TLS certificate fingerprint (sha256) ``` ```text $ node openclaw.mjs node install --help OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (dfb3581) ‚Äî All your chats, one OpenClaw. Usage: openclaw node install [options] Install the node host service (launchd/systemd/schtasks) Options: --context-path Gateway WebSocket context path (e.g. /openclaw-gw) ... ``` ### Unit tests ‚Äî 5 new test cases `src/node-host/runner.test.ts`: ``` ‚úì appends context path to the Gateway WebSocket URL ‚úì preserves trailing slash in context path as-is ‚úì prepends leading slash when context path is missing one ‚úì omits context path when empty or undefined ‚úì reads context path from saved node.json when not passed via CLI ‚úì CLI context path overrides saved node.json value ``` All 8 tests pass (3 existing + 5 new). ## User Impact Nodes can now connect to gateways behind path-routed reverse proxies. Backward compatible ‚Äî existing `node run` invocations without `--context-path` behave identically.
target.author: wm0018

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: feat(node): add --context-path flag to node run/install for reverse-p‚Ä¶
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves When an OpenClaw gateway is exposed through a reverse proxy (nginx, Caddy, Cloudflare Tunnel, etc.), the WebSocket endpoint often lives at a path like `wss://gateway.example.com/openclaw-gw`. Currently `openclaw node run` only accepts `--host` and `--port`, assembling the URL as `ws://host:port` with no path component. Remote nodes cannot connect to gateways behind path-routed proxies. Closes #97678 ## Evidence ### Problem ‚Äî URL assembly before this change `src/node-host/runner.ts:264` (before): ```ts const url = `${scheme}://${host}:${port}`; ``` No way to add a path prefix. ### Solution ‚Äî `--context-path` flag on `node run` and `node install` ```bash # Gateway at wss://gateway.example.com:18789/openclaw-gw openclaw node run --host gateway.example.com --port 18789 --tls --context-path /openclaw-gw # Gateway at ws://192.168.1.100:18789/gw openclaw node run --host 192.168.1.100 --port 18789 --context-path /gw ``` ### URL assembly ‚Äî after `src/node-host/runner.ts`: ```ts const contextPath = gateway.contextPath ? \^[[2mstateVersion\^[[22m=8","_meta":{"runtime":"node","runtimeVersion":"22.22.0","hostname":"LIN-4BF164C8B61.zte.intra","name":"{\"subsystem\":\"gateway/ws\"}","parentNames":["openclaw"],"date":"2026-06-29T11:02:24.763Z","logLevelId":3,"logLevelName":"INFO","path":{"fullFilePath":"file:///media/vdc/source/openclaw/dist/subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js:179:14","fileName":"subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js","fileNameWithLine":"subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js:179","fileColumn":"14","fileLine":"179","filePath":"dist/subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js","filePathWithLine":"dist/subsystem-yNfG7O3v.js:179","method":"logToFile"}},"time":"2026-06-29T19:02:24.763+08:00","hostname":"LIN-4BF164C8B61.zte.intra","message":"\^[[96m‚Üí\^[[39m \^[[1mhello-ok\^[[22m \^[[2mmethods\^[[22m=191 \^[[2mevents\^[[22m=27 \^[[2mpresence\^[[22m=2 \^[[2mstateVersion\^[[22m=8","traceId":"89a00682879c6a256d049dc20523674c","spanId":" ``` ### Unit tests ‚Äî 5 new test cases `src/node-host/runner.test.ts`: ``` ‚úì appends context path to the Gateway WebSocket URL ‚úì preserves trailing slash in context path as-is ‚úì prepends leading slash when context path is missing one ‚úì omits context path when empty or undefined ‚úì reads context path from saved node.json when not passed via CLI ‚úì CLI context path overrides saved node.json value ``` All 8 tests pass (3 existing + 5 new). ### Files changed | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/node-host/runner.ts` | URL assembly + persistence | | `src/node-host/config.ts` | `NodeHostGatewayConfig` type | | `src/cli/node-cli/register.ts` | `--context-path` flag on `run` + `install` | | `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` | Daemon install resolves contextPath | | `src/daemon/program-args.ts` | Daemon CLI arg persistence | | `src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.ts` | Pass contextPath through install plan | | `src/node-host/runner.test.ts` | 5 new tests | | `docs/cli/node.md` | Document flag on `run` + `install` | ### CLI help output ‚Äî `--context-path` visible on both commands ```text $ node openclaw.mjs node run --help OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (dfb3581) ‚Äî All your chats, one OpenClaw. Usage: openclaw node run [options] Run the headless node host (foreground) Options: --context-path Gateway WebSocket context path (e.g. /openclaw-gw) --display-name Override node display name -h, --help Display help for command --host Gateway host --node-id Override node id (clears pairing token) --port Gateway port --tls Use TLS for the gateway connection --tls-fingerprint Expected TLS certificate fingerprint (sha256) ``` ```text $ node openclaw.mjs node install --help OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (dfb3581) ‚Äî All your chats, one OpenClaw. Usage: openclaw node install [options] Install the node host service (launchd/systemd/schtasks) Options: --context-path Gateway WebSocket context path (e.g. /openclaw-gw) ... ``` ### Unit tests ‚Äî 5 new test cases `src/node-host/runner.test.ts`: ``` ‚úì appends context path to the Gateway WebSocket URL ‚úì preserves trailing slash in context path as-is ‚úì prepends leading slash when context path is missing one ‚úì omits context path when empty or undefined ‚úì reads context path from saved node.json when not passed via CLI ‚úì CLI context path overrides saved node.json value ``` All 8 tests pass (3 existing + 5 new). ## User Impact Nodes can now connect to gateways behind path-routed reverse proxies. Backward compatible ‚Äî existing `node run` invocations without `--context-path` behave identically.
